title: Explicit Construction of Short Minimal Codes
statement_text: Give an explicit construction of $\mathbb{F}_q$-linear minimal codes of length $n$ and dimension $k$ such that $n = O(qk)$, i.e., $n$ is at most linear in $k$ for fixed $q$.
context_text: Any minimal code of length $n$ and dimension $k$ over $\mathbb{F}_q$ must satisfy $n \geq (q+1)(k-1)$. While a probabilistic argument establishes the existence of minimal codes with $n$ linear in $k$, giving good explicit constructions has been identified as a challenging and central open problem in the area. Such codes with $n$ linear in $k$ yield asymptotically good error-correcting codes.
attributed_to: Alfarano, Borello, Neri et al. (inferred from [21])
paper_contribution: Provides the first explicit construction of strong blocking sets of size $O(qk)$ in $\mathrm{PG}(k-1,q)$ for every prime power $q$, yielding the first explicit $k$-dimensional minimal $\mathbb{F}_q$-linear code of length $n \leq cqk$ for an absolute constant $c$; the construction improves the previous best explicit constructions for every fixed $q \geq 7$.
notes: The paper's abstract states this 'solves one of the main open problems on minimal codes.' Reference [21] is not resolved from the provided text, so attribution is approximate. PDF source — math notation appears clean.
paper_title: Strong blocking sets and minimal codes from expander graphs
